_comment: Curated Malaysia-national doorstep issues salient to the 2026 Johor vote — same discipline as issues.json: dated, receipted, sourced, verdict-flagged. VERIFICATION METHOD DISCLOSURE: this environment's egress blocks direct page loads of news/gov sites, so every fact was corroborated via multiple independent search-result excerpts tied to the cited URLs (finder pass + a second independent search pass), not full on-page reads — hence PARTLY_CONFIRMED rather than VERIFIED. Re-verify against the live pages before printing campaign material. Each issue's `theme` maps to a muda_stances.json theme so the MUDA angle can be overlaid in the muda edition.

issues.issue_bm: Subsidi petrol kini bersasar: RM1.99 seliter dengan MyKad sejak 30 September 2025 — tetapi kuota bulanan dipotong daripada 300 kepada 200 liter pada 1 April 2026, dan harga pasaran RON95 tanpa subsidi mencecah RM3.37 seliter pada minggu kempen ini, sementara BUDI Diesel RM2.10 yang bermula 1 Julai berkongsi kuota 200 liter yang sama.
issues.issue_en: Petrol subsidy is now targeted: RM1.99 a litre with MyKad since 30 September 2025 — but the monthly quota was cut from 300 to 200 litres on 1 April 2026, the unsubsidised market price of RON95 hit RM3.37 a litre in this campaign week, and the new BUDI Diesel RM2.10 scheme that started 1 July shares the same 200-litre quota.
issues.receipt_bm: MOF (29 Sep 2025): BUDI95 bermula penuh 30 Sep — RM1.99 seliter melalui pengesahan MyKad, harga tanpa subsidi RM2.60 sebelum apungan mingguan. The Star (26 Mac 2026): subsidi BUDI95+BUDI Diesel ~RM4 bilion sebulan selepas minyak mentah melepasi USD100; PM umum kuota dipotong 300→200 liter mulai 1 April. MOF (22 Jun 2026): BUDI Madani Diesel RM2.10 seliter seluruh negara mulai 1 Julai 2026, kuota 200 liter dikongsi dengan BUDI95. paultan.org (30 Jun 2026): harga minggu 1-8 Julai — RON95 tanpa subsidi RM3.37, RON97 RM4.00, diesel RM3.97.
issues.receipt_en: MOF (29 Sep 2025): BUDI95 full rollout 30 Sep — RM1.99/litre via MyKad verification, unsubsidised price RM2.60 before moving to a weekly float. The Star (26 Mar 2026): BUDI95+BUDI Diesel subsidies hit ~RM4 billion a month after crude passed USD100; the PM announced the quota cut from 300 to 200 litres from 1 April. MOF (22 Jun 2026): BUDI Madani Diesel at RM2.10/litre nationwide from 1 July 2026, sharing the 200-litre quota with BUDI95. paultan.org (30 Jun 2026): 1-8 July week — unsubsidised RON95 RM3.37, RON97 RM4.00, diesel RM3.97.

issues.issue_bm: Sejak 1 Julai 2025 SST diperluas — cukai jualan 5-10% ke atas barangan bukan asas dan cukai perkhidmatan 6-8% ke atas SEWAAN, penjagaan kesihatan dan pendidikan swasta, pembinaan dan kewangan — dijangka mengutip ~RM10 bilion setahun menjelang 2026; namun pada musim panas yang SAMA, cukai Barangan Bernilai Tinggi (HVGT) yang dirancang ke atas golongan ultra-kaya dibatalkan terus (30 Julai 2025). Mereka meluaskan cukai ke atas hidup harian anda, dan membatalkan cukai ke atas orang kaya.
issues.issue_en: Since 1 July 2025 the SST has been expanded — 5-10% sales tax on non-essentials and 6-8% service tax on RENT, private healthcare and private education, construction and finance — expected to raise ~RM10 billion a year by 2026; yet in the SAME summer the planned High-Value Goods Tax on the ultra-rich was scrapped outright (30 July 2025). They widened the tax on your everyday life and cancelled the tax on the rich.
issues.receipt_bm: Malay Mail (9 Jun 2025): skop dan kadar perluasan SST mulai 1 Julai (barangan asas kekal 0%). Malay Mail (24 Jul 2025): MOF jangka tambahan RM5 bilion pada 2025 dan kira-kira dua kali ganda pada 2026. Malay Mail (30 Jul 2025): kerajaan TIDAK meneruskan HVGT (5-10% ke atas kereta >RM200k, jam tangan >RM20k, barang kemas >RM10k; ~RM700 juta hasil dilepaskan) — bersandar pada SST diperluas. Bernama (20 Jun 2025): tarif asas purata Semenanjung 45.40 sen/kWj mulai 1 Julai 2025; pengguna domestik ≤1,000kWj tidak terjejas.
issues.receipt_en: Malay Mail (9 Jun 2025): the SST expansion's scope and rates from 1 July (essentials stay zero-rated). Malay Mail (24 Jul 2025): MOF expects an extra RM5 billion in 2025 and roughly double in 2026. Malay Mail (30 Jul 2025): the government will NOT proceed with the HVGT (5-10% on cars >RM200k, watches >RM20k, jewellery >RM10k; ~RM700m forgone) — leaning on the expanded SST instead. Bernama (20 Jun 2025): Peninsular average base tariff 45.40 sen/kWh from 1 July 2025; domestic users ≤1,000kWh unaffected.

issues.issue_bm: Separuh pekerja Malaysia berpendapatan bawah ~RM2,800 sebulan (gaji penengah RM2,793, 2024) — sementara gaji penengah di Singapura kira-kira 6-7 kali ganda dalam ringgit, sebab itu 300,000+ rakyat merentas Tambak setiap hari. 1 daripada 3 graduan bekerja di bawah kelayakan (32.2%), dan bagi bawah 24 tahun ia 66%. Gaji minimum dinaikkan ke RM1,700 (Feb 2025), tetapi ia tidak menutup jurang yang membuatkan anak muda kita pergi.
issues.issue_en: Half of working Malaysians earn under ~RM2,800/month (median wage RM2,793, 2024) — while Singapore's median paycheck is roughly 6-7× that in ringgit, which is why 300,000+ cross the causeway daily. 1 in 3 graduates works below their qualification (32.2%); for under-24s it is 66%. The minimum wage rose to RM1,700 (Feb 2025), but it doesn't close the gap that makes our young leave.
issues.receipt_bm: DOSM Laporan Gaji & Upah 2024: gaji penengah bulanan RM2,793. Singapore MOM: gaji penengah kasar S$5,500 (pertengahan 2024) ≈ RM18,900 — kira-kira 6-7 kali ganda penengah Malaysia. CNBC (29 Okt 2024): 300,000+ rakyat Malaysia berulang-alik ke Singapura setiap hari, gaji lazimnya 3 kali lebih tinggi. OpenDOSM (2024): kadar kurang-guna berkaitan kemahiran graduan 32.2%; bawah-24 tahun 66%. The Star (18 Okt 2024): gaji minimum RM1,700 mulai 1 Feb 2025.
issues.receipt_en: DOSM Salaries & Wages Report 2024: monthly median wage RM2,793. Singapore MOM: median gross income S$5,500 (mid-2024) ≈ RM18,900 — roughly 6-7× Malaysia's median. CNBC (29 Oct 2024): 300,000+ Malaysians commute to Singapore daily for pay typically 3× higher. OpenDOSM (2024): graduate skills-related underemployment 32.2%; under-24s 66%. The Star (18 Oct 2024): minimum wage RM1,700 from 1 Feb 2025.

issues.issue_bm: Rumah di Malaysia tergolong 'seriously unaffordable' — harga penengah 4.72 kali pendapatan tahunan (2020); kerajaan janji sejuta rumah mampu milik menjelang 2035 dan tambah RM10 bilion jaminan pinjaman SJKP, tetapi tekanan harga dirasai SEKARANG, bukan 2035.
issues.issue_en: Malaysian housing is 'seriously unaffordable' — median price 4.72 times annual income (2020); the government pledges a million affordable homes by 2035 and an extra RM10 billion in SJKP loan guarantees, but the price pressure is being felt NOW, not in 2035.
issues.receipt_bm: DOSM/The Edge (2022): nisbah harga-pendapatan 4.72x pada 2020 — dalam kelompok 'seriously unaffordable' (4.1-5.0) kaedah Median Multiple. Malay Mail (31 Jul 2025): PM janji sejuta rumah mampu milik 2026-2035 bawah RMK-13. The Star (29 Okt 2025): tambahan RM10 bilion untuk Skim Jaminan Kredit Perumahan dalam Belanjawan 2026 (pembeli pertama tanpa slip gaji tetap, termasuk pekerja gig).
issues.receipt_en: DOSM/The Edge (2022): house price-to-income ratio 4.72x in 2020 — inside the 'seriously unaffordable' band (4.1-5.0) on the Median Multiple method. Malay Mail (31 Jul 2025): the PM pledged one million affordable homes 2026-2035 under the 13th Malaysia Plan. The Star (29 Oct 2025): an extra RM10 billion for the SJKP housing credit guarantee scheme in Budget 2026 (first-time buyers without fixed payslips, including gig workers).
